newsArray.title: From World Champions to a losing run! Is India coach Gautam Gambhir repeating Greg Chappell’s biggest mistake? All you need to know
newsArray.description: After losing to Ireland, India’s embarrassing 0-3 defeat against England has made a mockery of the T20 World Champions. Head coach Gautam Gambhir’s overconfidence seems to be causing the team’s rapid decline. The post From World Champions to a losing run! Is India coach Gautam Gambhir repeating Greg Chappell’s biggest mistake? All you need to know appeared first on News24 .
creator: akshat jain

newsArray.title: Zimbabwe confirm future captains across all formats
newsArray.description: HARARE, July 10 : Zimbabwe have confirmed Richard Ngarava will captain their test and One-Day International sides while veteran Sikandar Raza is retained as Twenty20 captain, the country’s cricket board said in a statement on Friday.Josephine Nkomo was appointed captain of the Zimbabwe women’s team.Ngarava made his captaincy debut in the one-off Test against Bangladesh at Harare Sports Club last month, which Zimbabwe won by an innings and 85 runs, and was also in charge for their two ODI wins over Bangladesh this week.The 28-year-old opening bowler replaces Craig Ervine as test captain.(Writing by Mark Gleeson in Atlanta; Editing by Toby Chopra)

newsArray.title: NGT Halts All Sports Activities At 3 Major Stadiums For Water Violations; IPL 2027 Venues Affected
newsArray.description:
The National Green Tribunal has banned sports activities at three prominent Indian cricket stadiums for failing to address environmental concerns. The interim directive follows the refusal of the stadium managements to respond to legal notices regarding their water procurement methods. The investigation targets the unsustainable extraction of groundwater to prepare playing surfaces during local shortages.

Administrative Silence Triggers Legal Sanctions

The enforcement order impacts Mumbai’s DY Patil Stadium, Jaipur’s Sawai Mansingh Stadium, and Raipur’s Shaheed Veer Narayan Singh International Stadium. These venues ignored multiple warnings from both the Green Tribunal and the Central Ground Water Authority. Consequently, the bench stopped all athletic operations until the associations present formal explanations.

The legal action stems from an execution application filed by applicant Haider Ali against structural water mismanagement. The original petition objected to authorities using commercial drinking water resources to irrigate cricket outfields. The tribunal mandates that state associations must substitute fresh water with treated effluent from local treatment facilities.

ALSO READ | WATCH: Gautam Gambhir Confronts Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Live On Camera Post T20I Loss

Furthermore, the targeted cricket grounds failed to implement efficient rainwater harvesting storage systems. These systems are legally required to facilitate essential aquifer recharge during monsoon periods. The environmental panel noted that sports bodies cannot continue commercial entertainment operations while depleting critical civic reserves.

Selective Compliance Spares Competitor Venues

The environmental tribunal had previously served identical closure warnings to six national cricket venues in April. However, three international facilities managed to avoid the operational ban by communicating with the regulatory agency. Their legal representatives submitted preliminary compliance details to the central authority before the deadline. New Delhi’s Arun Jaitley Stadium and Lucknow’s Ekana Cricket Stadium submitted formal responses. The capital venue is currently compiling additional consumption statistics requested by federal environmental inspectors. Meanwhile, Barabati Stadium in Cuttack secured a four-week extension to present its complete infrastructure plan. Counsel representing the applicant established that the three suspended venues repeatedly refused to participate in the hearings. Financial penalties failed to force the state cricket associations to disclose their primary water sources. The absolute lack of institutional engagement left the tribunal with no alternative but closure.

Judicial Focus On Domestic Water Security

In the official ruling, NGT Chairperson Justice Prakash Shrivastava highlighted the severe water deficits affecting multiple domestic sectors. The chairperson explained the reasoning behind the immediate suspensions by pointing out the uncooperative stance of the venues. The administration expected sports bodies to implement immediate corrective engineering adjustments.

The official statement from the National Green Tribunal confirmed the finality of the operational freeze:

"Having regard to the serious issue of water shortage in different areas of the country, we were expecting that these stadiums will duly respond to the tribunal's notice and will take appropriate corrective measures, but in spite of repeated notices not only from the tribunal but also from the CGWA, these three stadiums have not cared to file any response. Therefore, in such circumstances, by way of interim direction, we restrain the following three stadiums -- Shaheed Veer Narayan Singh International Stadium, Raipur; Sawai Mansingh Stadium, Jaipur; and D Y Patil Stadium, Mumbai -- from carrying on any further sports activity in the stadium without the leave of the tribunal till the next date of hearing."

ALSO READ | Virat Kohli And Rohit Sharma Forced To Step Up To Save Tour After Humiliation In T20Is

The suspension threatens domestic cricket schedules and state training programmes in Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh, and Rajasthan. Ground management teams must now modify their irrigation systems before the next formal review. The regulatory body has scheduled the next comprehensive case evaluation for 17 August 2026.

Long-Term Ramifications For IPL 2027

The immediate closure introduces profound administrative uncertainty ahead of the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2027 season. The affected venues feature prominently in prime domestic planning, with Jaipur serving as the primary base for the Rajasthan Royals franchise. Any prolonged operational ban threatens to force logistically complex relocations of high-revenue matches next spring.

The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) must closely monitor compliance progression before formalizing the tournament itinerary. Franchise owners require long-term venue certainty regarding pitch conditioning and grandstand hospitality preparations. Stadium governing bodies must swiftly restructure their entire plumbing design to satisfy federal environmental oversight boards.
creator: prateek thakur

newsArray.title: India News | NGT Restrains Three Cricket Stadiums from Hosting Sports Activities over Non-compliance in Groundwater Conservation Case
newsArray.description: Get latest articles and stories on India at LatestLY. The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has restrained three major cricket stadiums Shaheed Veer Narayan Singh International Stadium in Raipur, Sawai Mansingh Stadium in Jaipur, and Dr DY Patil Stadium in Mumbai from conducting any sports activities without the Tribunal's permission after they repeatedly failed to respond to its directions in a long-pending matter concerning groundwater conservation and the use of treated sewage water for stadium maintenance.

newsArray.title: BCCI To Assess India's White-Ball Setbacks After England And Ireland Disappointments
newsArray.description: The BCCI will hold a review meeting with Team India's core members after the ODI series ends on July 19 to assess the side's poor T20I performances in England and Ireland. Secretary Devajit Saikia told ANI the discussion will focus only on on-field shortcomings and course correction, describing the team's struggles as a "purely bad phase".

newsArray.title: India News | NGT Restrains Three Cricket Stadiums from Hosting Sports Activities over Non-compliance in Groundwater Conservation Case
newsArray.description: Get latest articles and stories on India at LatestLY. The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has restrained three major cricket stadiums Shaheed Veer Narayan Singh International Stadium in Raipur, Sawai Mansingh Stadium in Jaipur, and Dr DY Patil Stadium in Mumbai from conducting any sports activities without the Tribunal's permission after they repeatedly failed to respond to its directions in a long-pending matter concerning groundwater conservation and the use of treated sewage water for stadium maintenance.

newsArray.description: The NGT has banned three stadiums from sports activities due to non-compliance with groundwater conservation directives, highlighting serious environmental concerns.

newsArray.title: BCCI To Assess India's White-Ball Setbacks After England And Ireland Disappointments
newsArray.description: The BCCI will hold a review meeting with Team India's core members after the ODI series ends on July 19 to assess the side's poor T20I performances in England and Ireland. Secretary Devajit Saikia told ANI the discussion will focus only on on-field shortcomings and course correction, describing the team's struggles as a "purely bad phase".
